title: Lead generation expert
description:
Role Overview

As a Lead Generation expert, you will be responsible for generating high-quality leads and setting new business opportunities for our recruitment consultants. You’ll thrive in a fast-paced environment that rewards persistence, strategic outreach, and measurable results. This is a high-activity role focused on outreach volume, client engagement, and delivering consistent proposal pipelines each week.



Key Responsibilities

Conduct high-volume outbound outreach through
, phone, and LinkedIn to engage potential clients in target industries.



Generate and qualify leads for recruitment partnerships and project-based hiring solutions.



Build tailored proposals and prospect communications to position Emerge Tech’s services effectively.



Collaborate closely with the Business Development and Recruitment teams to optimise lead-to-client conversion rates.



Manage prospect data and outreach activity through CRM and lead management tools.



Meet and exceed weekly and monthly KPIs around outreach, proposals, and qualified opportunities created.



Represent Emerge Tech’s brand with professionalism and consultative insight at every touchpoint.



Requirements

3+ years of experience in a sales, SDR, or lead generation role (recruitment or tech sector experience preferred).



Strong written and verbal communication skills — confident engaging senior decision-makers.



Ability to manage high activity levels while maintaining quality outreach.



Proficiency with CRM software, prospecting tools, and LinkedIn Sales Navigator.



Competitive mindset with a passion for hitting goals and seeing direct results from your efforts.



What We Offer

Competitive base salary plus industry-leading commission structure with uncapped potential.

Clear progression routes into senior business development or recruitment consultant roles.

Supportive environment with regular sales training and performance coaching.

Dynamic, high-performance culture where results are recognised and rewarded.

title: Expedited Frieght Operations Coordinator
description:
Freight Operations Coordinator (Expedited / High-Touch Logistics)



We are a U.S.-based freight brokerage that works exclusively with freight forwarders, specializing in expedited, high-priority shipments.



This is a high-touch, high-responsibility role. We handle time-sensitive freight where execution, communication, and documentation must be precise.



We are looking for someone with direct freight forwarding or brokerage experience who can step in and operate with minimal training.



Core Responsibilities

Track shipments from pickup through final delivery

Communicate proactively with customers and carriers:

Driver on-site notifications

Loaded / in-transit updates

Delivery confirmation + POD turnaround

Ensure real-time tracking compliance (MacroPoint or similar)

Coordinate with carriers via phone, text, and

Identify and resolve delays or service failures quickly

Maintain accurate and organized shipment records

Documentation & Compliance (Critical)



You must have experience handling:



Proof of Delivery (POD) retrieval and submission (time-sensitive)

Freight forwarding workflows and communication standards

Import documentation

Border crossing documentation (ACE, customs coordination, etc.)



This role requires someone who understands how freight moves—not someone learning from scratch.



What This Role Requires

Fast response time (minutes, not hours)

Comfort making outbound calls to drivers and dispatchers

Ability to manage multiple shipments without losing detail

Strong sense of urgency and ownership

Clear, professional communication with U.S.-based customers

Required Experience

Freight forwarding or freight brokerage experience (required)

Experience working with freight forwarders (required)

Experience with expedited or time-sensitive shipments (required)

Familiarity with:

Sprinter vans / straight trucks / dry vans

Drayage (preferred)

Experience with tracking tools (MacroPoint, FourKites, etc.)

Compensation

Competitive monthly pay (based on experience)

Performance-based bonuses tied to execution and reliability

Important



This is not a general virtual assistant role.

This is a high-performance operations role within a specialized logistics business.



To Apply



Please include:



Your experience working with freight forwarders

Your experience with PODs, import or border documentation

A brief example of a shipment issue you handled

Your internet speed and work setup



???? Start your application with the word “TRACKING” so we know you read this.

title: Help Desk Support - Level 1 (Remote, Full-Time)
description:
We’re looking for a customer-focused Level 1 Help Desk Support Analyst to serve as the front line of support for our managed services clients. This role is centered on professional client interaction, accurate ticket intake, and efficient management of a shared MSP ticket queue supporting multiple organizations.

This is an excellent entry-level opportunity for someone starting their IT career who enjoys helping users, follows process, and thrives in a fast-paced, service-driven environment.



Who We Are

We’re a managed services provider (MSP) delivering reliable IT support to a diverse client base. Our success depends on consistent communication, clear documentation, and disciplined execution across every client interaction.



Our Culture



Client Focused Service

We support multiple businesses, each with their own expectations and SLAs. Professionalism, empathy, and clarity are essential.



Process Driven Support

Standardization matters in an MSP. We follow defined workflows for intake, triage, escalation, and resolution.



Collaborative Team Environment

Level 1 support works closely with Level 2 and Level 3 technicians to ensure issues are routed correctly and resolved quickly.



Remote First and Accountable

We trust our tea
mbers to manage their workload, meet SLAs, and communicate proactively in a remote setting.



How to Apply (Please Read Carefully)

To be considered, you must submit the following through the job platform’s messaging system:

• A link to a short introductory video explaining why you're a strong candidate

• Your resume

• Your profile link on this job platform

Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.



Key Responsibilities

• Act as the first point of contact for MSP clients via phone, chat, and

• Represent our company professionally to end users across multiple client environments

• Create, document, and update tickets with clear, accurate, and complete information

• Monitor and manage the MSP ticket queue, ensuring tickets are properly categorized, prioritized, and assigned

• Perform basic troubleshooting using client-specific documentation and knowledge base articles

• Resolve common Level 1 issues or escalate to Level 2/3 support following MSP escalation standards

• Communicate status updates and next steps clearly to clients

• Meet defined SLAs, response times, and customer satisfaction targets

• Assist in maintaining client documentation and internal knowledge base articles



Required Qualifications

• 1–2 years of help desk, MSP, or customer-facing technical support experience

• Strong verbal and written English communication skills

• Proven customer service mindset with professional phone etiquette

• Experience working with a ticketing system in a multi-client environment

• Ability to follow standardized procedures and client-specific workflows

• Strong organization skills and ability to manage multiple tickets at once

• Reliability and consistency in meeting schedules and service metrics



Basic Technical Knowledge (Expected)

• General familiarity with Windows and/or macOS workstations

• Basic experience with Microsoft 365 applications (Outlook, Teams, OneDrive)

• Understanding of common MSP tasks such as:



Password resets

Account access issues

Basic application and connectivity troubleshooting

• Ability to gather accurate technical information for escalation



Preferred (Nice to Have)

• Prior experience working for an MSP

• Familiarity with RMM + PSA / ticketing tools (Gorelo and Syncro)

• CompTIA A+ or other entry-level IT certification

• Experience supporting multiple clients with varying environments



Work From Home Requirements

• Quiet, dedicated workspace suitable for client calls

• Reliable computer with current hardware

• Stable high?speed internet connection

• Professional headset with microphone

• Webcam for meetings, training, and team check-ins



Growth Opportunity

This MSP Level 1 role is designed as a foundation position, with opportunities to advance into Level 2 support, NOC, SOC, or specialized technical roles based on performance and skill growth.



Ready to Apply?

If you enjoy working with clients, thrive in structured environments, and want to build a career in managed IT services, we’d love to hear from you. Submit your application with all required materials listed above.

title: Remote Productivity Coach - No Experience Needed. Help People Reach Their Goals
description:
*No coaching experience needed- training provided.*



We're a growing productivity coaching app helping people build better habits and reach their goals. We're looking for empathetic, consistent people who genuinely want to help others grow.



Your role:

• Check in daily via text with each client to track progress and keep them accountable

• Proactively encourage, challenge, and guide clients- not just cheer them on, but help them problem-solve and stay on track

• Build genuine connections through consistent, thoughtful communication

• Make occasional short video calls to check in with clients



What you'll be part of:

• A supportive team of 15+ coaches who genuinely care about helping people grow

• Weekly team calls where coaches discuss strategies, share insights, and learn together

• Monthly 20 min reads on coaching and personal development topics that we discuss as a team

• A culture of sharing a bit of who you are- eg: posting a casual photo or a quick life update to build connection with clients (think low-key and natural, not too personal)

• Regular feedback on your coaching to help you keep growing



About training:

Before working with clients, you'll complete a structured 10-day training program. Expect to dedicate approximately 1.5–2 hours per day during training. This includes reading our coaching methodology, completing practical exercises with feedback, live practice sessions, and using the app daily as both a coach and a client. We invest in thorough training because quality matters- coaches who complete it feel confident and well-prepared from day one.



Schedule:

• You choose your working schedule (pick a time block and stick to it)

• Daily check-ins include weekends (workload is lighter on weekends)

• Consistency is key- clients rely on hearing from you at roughly the same time each day



*This is a committed long term role, not a casual freelance gig- we're looking for people who want to stick around and grow with us*



If you enjoy connecting with people and helping them grow, we'd love to hear from you!





?? To apply, please answer these questions in your message:



1. What is your preferred 3-hour work block? (UTC)

• 8–11 PM (highest priority)

• 5–8 PM or 3–6 PM (high priority)

• 2–11 AM, 11 AM–2 PM or other (lowest)



2. Daily check-ins at the same time every day (incl. weekends — lighter workload) + a 10-day training program (1.5–2 hrs/day) are required. Can you commit to both?



3. Alex, a developer, says: "I'm overwhelmed with client projects, marketing, social media… I get sidetracked easily, and by the time I deal with
s, it's too late for my core work. Staying motivated is getting tough." How would you reply? (Don't use AI)



4. Sam has been checking in with you daily for two weeks and making good progress. Then he goes silent for 3 days — no check-ins, no messages. What do you do? (Don't use AI)



5. Do you have a computer and reliable internet access?



Looking forward to your answers!

title: Medical Administrative Assistant - QuickBooks & Billing Support
description:
Job Title: Medical Administrative Assistant – QuickBooks & Billing Support

Work Hours: 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM CDT

Work Days: Monday – Friday





Scope of Work / Responsibilities

We are looking for a detail-oriented and reliable Medical Administrative Assistant to support a growing consulting business with bookkeeping, invoicing, and administrative tasks.

This role is ideal for someone with strong QuickBooks experience, Excel skills, and billing knowledge, who can help streamline financial processes and ensure accurate tracking of billable hours.

The goal is to help the business owner save time, stay organized, and ensure accurate and timely client billing.



Core Responsibilities

1. QuickBooks Management (Primary Priority)

Maintain and update financial records in QuickBooks

Perform data entry and transaction categorization

Ensure accurate and organized bookkeeping

Support general financial record-keeping



2. Invoice Generation & Billing

Create and send professional invoices to clients

Manage billing for both flat-fee and hourly-based services

Ensure invoices are accurate and sent on time



3. Billable Hour Tracking

Track and log billable hours accurately

Maintain organized records of time worked

Ensure all billable work is captured for proper client billing

Support general administrative tasks as needed

Maintain spreadsheets and reports using Excel

Assist with organizing financial and client records

Coordinate tasks based on business needs and travel schedule



Systems & Tools Used

QuickBooks (Required)

Microsoft Excel / Google Sheets

General administrative tools

ECW (nice to have, but not required)





Experience Requirements

Previous experience as a Virtual Assistant or Administrative Assistant preferred

Strong experience with QuickBooks (required)

Experience with invoice creation and billing workflows required

Healthcare background preferred but not required

HIPAA Certification required



Required Skills

QuickBooks Expertise (Non-Negotiable)

Billing & Invoicing Experience

Experience managing multiple billing models (flat fee + hourly)

Strong attention to detail in financial documentation



Excel Proficiency

Comfortable managing spreadsheets and tracking data



Organization & Accuracy

Strong attention to detail

title: Delivery Executive
description:
Send your resume to 



WFH

Graveyard Shift

1 year contract required



***Role Overview

The Delivery Executive serves as the “Architect of Execution,” acting as the second-in-command responsible for translating the company’s strategic vision into actionable, scalable operational workflows. This role partners closely with the CEO to ensure the organization has the right people, processes, and systems in place to achieve sustainable growth and operational excellence.



***Key Responsibilities



Strategic Execution: Translate the CEO’s vision and company goals into clear, structured, and executable operational plans across all departments.



Operational Leadership: Oversee day-to-day operations, including service delivery, production, customer success, and internal support functions such as HR and IT.



Performance Management: Develop, implement, and monitor KPIs and OKRs to ensure teams consistently meet or exceed performance targets.



Financial Oversight: Work closely with the Finance team to manage budgets, optimize costs, and ensure efficient allocation of resources aligned with growth objectives.



Culture & Change Management: Lead organizational improvements and transformation initiatives while promoting a culture of accountability, performance, and continuous development.



Process Optimization: Identify operational inefficiencies and implement streamlined or automated solutions to improve productivity and service quality.



Cross-Functional Collaboration: Ensure alignment and seamless coordination between departments to support business objectives and enhance overall execution.



Client & Delivery Excellence: Maintain high standards of service delivery, ensuring client satisfaction, retention, and consistent operational performance.



***Qualifications



- Minimum of 5 years of experience in a senior leadership or operations role, preferably in a high-growth environment.



- Strong understanding of operational strategy, business processes, and organizational scaling.



- Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams and drive measurable results.



- Experience in performance management frameworks (KPIs, OKRs).



- Financial acumen with experience in budgeting and resource planning.



- Excellent communication, leadership, and stakeholder management skills.



- Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with a data-driven mindset.



-Industry experience in [e.g., BPO, SaaS, Logistics, etc.] is highly preferred.



***Success Metrics (First 12 Months)



- Achieve measurable efficiency improvements (e.g., reduce operational costs or increase productivity by at least 15%).



- Successfully scale operations within a key department or market segment.



- Improve service delivery performance and client satisfaction metrics.



- Maintain or improve employee retention and engagement levels.



- Implement at least one major process improvement or automation initiative.



Reporting Line

Reports directly to the CEO.

title: Senior WordPress Website Designer
description:
About Wise Agent

Wise Agent is a U.S.-based real estate CRM company headquartered in Fountain Hills, Arizona. For more than 20 years, we have helped real estate professionals stay organized, automate follow-up, and grow their business with less stress.

We are known for something rare in tech. Real people. Real support. Real relationships.

Wise Agent is expanding our Website Services division, building custom WordPress websites for real estate agents and brokers across the U.S.



About This Role

This role is for someone with serious WordPress experience, not someone still learning which plugin does what. We are looking for an experienced WordPress Website Designer who can build fast, clean, conversion-focused websites consistently and on deadline.

You will work closely with our U.S.-based team to design and build custom real estate websites using WordPress and Elementor. You will take a client’s vision, research their market and location, and turn that into a professional, SEO-friendly website that looks great and performs even better.

If you enjoy building sites that are polished, organized, and actually launch on time, and you are comfortable working with clear deadlines, daily check ins, and fast feedback cycles, you will fit in well here.



Key Responsibilities

Build and customize WordPress websites for real estate agents and brokers

Design pages using Elementor with clean layout, spacing, and hierarchy

Implement on-page SEO best practices including metadata, images, headings, and site structure

Optimize site speed, responsiveness, and mobile experience

Collaborate with internal team on content, structure, and revisions

Take feedback professionally and make revisions efficiently

Communicate progress clearly, respond promptly during work hours, and meet deadlines consistently

Be responsive in Slack during assigned work hours and communicate blockers early

Attend scheduled meetings on time and prepared

Provide regular progress updates without being asked

Maintain organized workflows and attention to detail across multiple projects



Required Skills and Experience

Strong, proven experience building WordPress websites

Deep knowledge of WordPress themes, plugins, and best practices

Advanced comfort using Elementor and Adobe tools such as Photoshop and Premiere Pro

Portfolio of live WordPress websites you personally built or designed

Experience optimizing content and structure for SEO

Strong eye for design, layout, spacing, and branding

Ability to design clean, professional graphics and imagery

Proficiency with Adobe tools and or Canva

Excellent English communication skills, written and verbal

High attention to detail and strong grammar skills

Ability to manage time effectively and deliver consistent output during assigned hours

Proven track record of completing projects on schedule with minimal supervision



Nice to Have

Experience building real estate or local business websites

Familiarity with video embeds using Vimeo

Ability to prompt and edit content using AI tools such as ChatGPT or Gemini

Basic understanding of website conversion best practices

Experience working with U.S.-based clients or companies



What We Are Really Looking For

Someone who does not need constant supervision

Someone who asks smart questions before building the wrong thing

Someone who takes pride in their work and notices the small details

Someone reliable. Impeccable attendance and on time meeting participation matter here

If you are frequently late to meetings, slow to respond during work hours, or need repeated follow up to stay on track, this role will not be a fit.

If you love building excellent websites and seeing them live, you will thrive here.



Work Style Expectations

This role requires consistent availability and communication during assigned work hours.

Slack is our primary communication tool and is expected to be monitored throughout the day

Questions and blockers should be raised early, not after deadlines slip

Website builds are expected to progress steadily each day, not in last minute bursts

Quality and speed both matter. Taking excessive time without visible progress will be addressed



What We Offer

Competitive USD-based pay

Long-term, stable remote work

Clear processes and realistic deadlines

Supportive, people-first company culture

Opportunity to grow with a trusted U.S. tech company



How to Apply

Please submit the following:

Your updated resume

Links to live WordPress websites you personally built

Any relevant design or portfolio links

Please confirm you are able to work Arizona business hours reliably.

Optional but highly encouraged:

A short Loom or video introduction telling us who you are, your WordPress experience, and why you would be a great fit for Wise Agent.

Applications without live WordPress portfolio links will not be reviewed.

, cold calling, and messaging** to start conversations

 tools

title: TCB: Junior Bookkeeper
description:
Position Title: Junior Bookkeeper

Company: Time Capital Bookkeeping

Reporting To: Thomas Chavez

Location: Remote

Hours: Full-time 40 hours per week.

Compensation: Offer starting at  $10/hr depending on experience and ability, with growth potential. Performance review after initial 90 days.

Start Date: March 1st

Application Window: Open until filled.

Please Apply here with this Google Form: 



About Us

Time Capital Bookkeeping provides decision-ready financials for real estate investors so they can move fast, stay focused, and grow profits. We are a client-obsessed, systems-driven firm that values simplicity, clear communication, critical thinking, and ownership.

Founded by Max Emory, a U.S. Marine Corps veteran and real estate investor, Time Capital was built to solve the exact problems he experienced firsthand—unclear numbers, wasted time, and overly complex financials. Today, we support real estate investors with accurate, reliable books they can confidently act on.



We live by our values:



Client-Obsessed

The client experience is the north star for decision-making. We prioritize accuracy, clarity, and responsiveness so clients can confidently act on their financials.



Simplicity

Complexity doesn’t scale. We aim for clear, repeatable systems that reduce friction for both clients and the internal team.



Speed & Communication

Issues are identified and addressed quickly through clear, proactive communication. Silence and delays create problems; clarity prevents them.



Extreme Ownership

We take responsibility for outcomes, even when it’s uncomfortable. Problems are owned, not passed.



Thinker, Not a Box Checker

We value problem-solvers who think critically and use judgment, not people who simply complete tasks without understanding the “why.”



Humility

A willingness to learn, ask questions, accept feedback, and seek understanding over ego.



Key Responsibilities 

Managing day-to-day bookkeeping for real estate investors and franchise/service-based businesses.

Reconciling bank and credit card accounts.

Handling accounts payable/receivable recordings and payroll entries

Building and maintaining a clean chart of accounts.

Producing accurate monthly financial reports (delivered by the 15th/20th).

Collaborating with the manager and team through Slack and Double (Keeper).

Ensuring compliance with deadlines and bookkeeping best practices.

Participating in cleanups, catch-up work, and diagnostics when needed.

Handling Intercompany Loans, Due To/Due From, etc

Managing multiple entities within each QBO account



Client Communication

Maintain consistent, professional communication with clients through Double (Keeper)

Track and follow up on required client documentation and access

Clearly communicate status updates, issues, and next steps internally



Process & Documentation

Follow established bookkeeping workflows

Document issues, edge cases, and process improvements

Support ongoing refinement of bookkeeping SOPs



Ideal Candidate Profile

The ideal candidate:

Has foundational bookkeeping knowledge or training

Has experience with real estate investing bookkeeping

Is comfortable working through historical financial data

Can follow detailed processes while thinking critically when exceptions arise

Communicates clearly and professionally

Is organized, detail-oriented, and accountable for outcomes

Is open to feedback and continuous learning



Preferred Qualifications: 

Basic working knowledge of real estate investing or real estate bookkeeping

1–3 years of bookkeeping experience preferred; candidates with less experience will be considered if they demonstrate strong critical thinking, accuracy, and ownership

Bookkeeper Launch certification or equivalent training preferred (may substitute for experience)

Fluent in English with clear written and verbal communication skills

Detail-oriented with a strong focus on accuracy and follow-through

Generally available Monday–Friday during normal business hours with strong EST overlap

Able to work 100% remotely with a reliable laptop/computer, high-speed internet, smartphone, and a quiet workspace



Required Qualifications

QBO Pro Advisor

Google Workspace (Drive, Gmail, etc).

Preferred: Keeper (now called Double)

Slack 

         

Full-Time Benefits: 

Work from home

Flexible Paid Time Off

Free continued education

Eligible for bonuses

Annual Home Office Stipend  



Our Policy on AI Tools 

We recognize that AI and translation tools are widely used by virtual assistants and remote professionals. We believe in being clear about what is acceptable and what is not for this role and interview process:



? Allowed & Encouraged

Drafting, brainstorming, or improving written communication 
s, SOPs, checklists, documentation).

Checking grammar, spelling, or tone for clarity.

Speeding up repetitive tasks or improving efficiency.

Using captions or transcription tools to follow meetings.



?? Limited / Not a Fit

Relying on translation tools for all real-time communication in English. This role requires strong listening comprehension and verbal English without constant translation.

Submitting AI-generated work without editing or adding your own input.

Hiding the use of AI when asked about your process.



???? What to Expect in Hiring

Written and verbal responses will be analyzed for AI-only content.

Interviews will assess live comprehension (your ability to follow spoken English in real time).

Supportive tools are fine—but we need to know you can communicate clearly and independently when required.



How to Apply

Please apply using the following Google Form: 



Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until the role is filled.

title: Junior Real Estate Bookkeeper (Remote – Philippines / Overseas VA)
description:
Third Little Pig Bookkeeping

Junior Real Estate Bookkeeper (Remote – Philippines / Overseas VA)

Part-Time | 10–15 hrs/week to start (15–20 as client load grows) | $10–$15/hr USD (experience-based) | Flexible Hours (Must overlap 9am–11pm EST)



About Third Little Pig Bookkeeping

Third Little Pig Bookkeeping exists to bring clarity and structural integrity to the financial lives of small business owners — from real estate investors and contractors to e-commerce entrepreneurs and service-based businesses. Founded in 2017 by Sal Sannuto, the firm was built on a simple belief: most bookkeeping is a mess, and it doesn't have to be.



Sal brings a rare combination of hands-on business experience — in real estate investment, e-commerce, and small business ownership — and meticulous attention to detail that most bookkeepers simply don't have. When you work with Third Little Pig, you're not getting a number-cruncher. You're getting a real person who understands your business, responds quickly, and actually cares whether your books are right.



We build the brick house — stable, accurate, and built to last.



We live by our values:

Integrity — We don't oversell, overpromise, or cut corners. What we say is what we do.

Accuracy — Clean reconciliations, proper classification, and structured documentation. The details matter because your decisions depend on them.

Responsiveness — Direct communication, fast replies, no ticket queues. Clients work with a real person who knows their business.

Simplicity — Standardized systems and repeatable workflows mean consistent results.



The Role

We are looking for a detail-oriented, self-directed Junior Real Estate Bookkeeper to join our growing remote team. This is a part-time contractor position starting at approximately 10–15 hours per week, growing to 15–20 hours as you get up to speed — with clear potential to become full-time as our client base expands.



You will work directly under the owner/founder, handling the day-to-day bookkeeping tasks for a portfolio of real estate investor clients — primarily using Xero — so the owner can focus on client relationships, business development, and strategic growth.



This is NOT just a task-taking role. We want someone who takes ownership, communicates proactively, and helps build a more efficient practice over time.



Core Responsibilities

Bookkeeping & Financial Accuracy

Record and categorize all transactions weekly for real estate investor clients (primarily cash-basis, with some accrual-basis clients due to invoicing).

Reconcile bank and credit card accounts across multiple clients and entities.

Track revenue and expenses by property, project, or portfolio.

Prepare and maintain accurate monthly financial reports and flag any discrepancies or concerns.

Handle fixed asset entries where applicable, including depreciation and project-specific CapEx tracking.

Review tax returns to validate reporting accuracy and support year-end preparation.

Manage installment sale reconciliation when relevant (knowledge in this area is a plus).

Client Portal & Communication Management (via Double HQ)

Log in to Double HQ daily and prioritize clearing the three key queues: (1) outstanding client questions / inbound queries, (2) pending client uploads, and (3) transaction questions awaiting client response.

Respond to client portal activity promptly — inbound client questions should be addressed the same day.

Escalate unresolved items or non-responsive clients to the owner in a timely manner with a clear log of outreach attempts.

Keep client files and task statuses updated and accurate in Double HQ at all times.



Systems & SOPs

Follow and maintain existing Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for financial workflows.

Suggest improvements to current accounting processes and documentation when you identify a better way.

Assist in building and refining client-specific templates within Double HQ as part of onboarding new accounts.



Communication & Collaboration

Communicate clearly and proactively with the owner via Slack — flag concerns early, don't wait.

Participate in weekly check-ins to review client statuses, outstanding items, and any escalations.

Complete Double HQ Academy training within your first week on the job.

Be a thought partner, not just a task-taker — if something doesn't look right, say so.



Tools We Use (or You'll Learn)

Xero (primary accounting software — proficiency required)

QuickBooks Online (a strong plus — we occasionally onboard QBO clients)

Double HQ (client dashboard and workflow management — training provided; complete Double HQ Academy in Week 1)

Google Workspace — Gmail, Google Drive, Google Sheets, Google Calendar, Google Meet

Slack (primary team communication)

LastPass (password management)

Loom (asynchronous video training and updates)

Gusto (payroll — at least one client uses this)





You're a Perfect Fit If…

You have at least 5+ years of professional bookkeeping experience, with at least 2 years specifically in real estate bookkeeping.

You are proficient in Xero — this is non-negotiable. QuickBooks Online experience is a strong bonus.

You understand cash-basis accounting for real estate investors and can manage multiple client entities cleanly.

You are comfortable with real estate-specific transactions: rental income, property purchases and sales, rehab costs, refinances, and depreciation entries.

You have knowledge of installment sale reconciliation (a significant plus for our client base).

You can confidently spot discrepancies, follow up on missing information, and produce clean monthly financials.

You are an excellent written and verbal English communicator — clear, kind, and professional. Strong English fluency is a firm requirement for this role.

You're highly organized and can manage multiple client accounts and deadlines without dropping the ball.

You build and improve bookkeeping systems — not just follow them.

You are comfortable working independently in a remote, async-first environment with a small, growing team.

You are a self-starter who takes ownership of your work and communicates proactively — not reactively.



Qualifications

5+ years of professional bookkeeping experience.

2+ years of bookkeeping experience specifically with real estate investors or real estate-adjacent businesses.

Xero proficiency (required). QuickBooks Online experience (strong plus).

Strong knowledge of GAAP and real estate bookkeeping best practices.

Xero Advisor Certification or equivalent is a plus.

Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills.

Strong English fluency (written and verbal) — this is a firm requirement.

Experience with property-level tracking, cash-basis bookkeeping, and multi-entity management.







Why Work With Us

Fully remote — work from anywhere (Philippines or overseas).

Flexible hours within a wide availability window (9am–11pm EST). We don't micromanage your schedule — we care about results.

Opportunity to grow with us — this role starts part-time and is designed to grow into a full-time position as our client base expands.

Clear SOPs, organized systems, and a supportive owner who is invested in your success and onboarding.

Weekly team check-ins to keep you connected, supported, and aligned.

Work with a focused, real estate-specialized bookkeeping firm — not a generalist shop.

Hourly rate based on experience: $10/hr for candidates with 1–3 years of real estate bookkeeping experience; $12–$15/hr for candidates with 5+ years and Xero certification.





Schedule & Availability

This is a part-time contractor role, approximately 10–15 hours per week to start, growing to 15–20 hours as you become familiar with clients and workflows.



Work hours are flexible within a wide window: 9:00am – 11:00pm Eastern Standard Time (EST).

You do not need to work exclusively during US business hours — most bookkeeping work is async and deadline-driven.

You are expected to be reachable via Slack and able to respond to client portal activity within the agreed same-day window.

You must be available for a weekly check-in meeting (time to be agreed upon at hire).



Note for Philippines-based applicants: Eastern Time is typically 12 hours behind Philippine Standard Time — evening/night hours PST are perfectly fine and expected.





How to Apply

We review every application carefully. We're looking for quality over speed - take your time and show us who you are.

Please submit your application here: 
 



What to expect in the application:

Written responses to questions - answer in your own words. We will check for AI-generated responses.

We're evaluating how you communicate, how you think, and whether you're the right fit for our team.

Shortlisted applicants will be invited to a video interview.



Timeline

Applications open until: Position filled

Interviews will be conducted: Beginning first week of April

Target start date: April 15, 2026

title: Senior Lead Generation And Email Marketing Specialist
description:
Description

Description

We are seeking a Senior Lead Generation Specialist who will be esponsible for designing, executing, and managing lead generation and referral development strategy. Oversees sourcing, research, outreach, qualification, and scheduling of pre-assessment appointments. Manages referral outreach campaigns to increase inbound and outbound referral opportunities. Supports up to the pre-assessment stage and, when needed, conducts pre-assessment calls to assist the Supervisor for Intake & Client Conversion. Ensures accurate documentation, disciplined follow-up, compliance, and strict protection of proprietary information.



Core Responsibilities: 



Develop and execute lead generation strategy to consistently produce warm, qualified prospects for intake and conversion.

Build and manage outreach campaigns targeting private-pay prospects and referral partners to increase lead flow and strengthen referral pipelines.

Source, research, organize, clean, segment, and manage prospect and referral lists for optimized outreach and conversion.

Conduct structured multi-channel outreach (calls, SMS,
) using approved messaging and compliance standards.

Qualify prospects based on service need, urgency, location, and suitability.

Nurture leads through structured follow-up cycles until ready for pre-assessment scheduling.

Schedule pre-assessment appointments directly with the Supervisor for Intake & Client Conversion and confirm details with clients.

When required, conduct pre-assessment calls to support the Supervisor for Intake & Client Conversion during highvolume or urgent periods.

Accurately update CRM stages, notes, tags, tasks, and communication logs immediately after every interaction.

Notify the Managing Member and Supervisor for Intake & Client Conversion when leads become warm, preassessments are booked, or conversions occur.

Produce weekly lead generation and referral performance reports including lead volume, warm leads, campaign effectiveness, and recommendations for improvement.

Drive outbound and referral campaign performance to generate the lead volume necessary to support growth trajectory.

Continuously refine outreach cadence, messaging, targeting, and list quality based on performance metrics and conversion outcomes.

Collaborate with the Supervisor for Intake & Client Conversion to ensure smooth hand-offs and optimal lead readiness for pre-assessment.

Collaborate with the Supervisor for HR Recruitment to align staffing needs with projected lead and referral volume.

Maintain communication flow through WhatsApp channels and ensure all appointments are updated accurately on internal calendars.

Identify declines in lead quality, referral engagement, or outreach performance early and provide corrective strategies.



Data Protection & Confidentiality Requirements:



May only work with leads sourced personally or assigned for outreach; proprietary leads, lists, or strategies may not be exported, saved, copied, shared, reused, or retained outside approved systems.

Breach of confidentiality, improper handling of data, or unauthorized retention of contact information results in immediate termination.



Performance Standards:



Production of high-quality warm leads and referral opportunities aligned with intake and service needs. • Strong conversion rates from outreach to qualified warm status.

CRM documentation must be complete, accurate, and updated in real time.

Reliable scheduling of pre-assessment appointments with confirmed client communication.

Weekly reporting delivered on time with actionable insights and performance analysis.

Professional, compliant, and consistent communication across all outreach efforts.

Requirements

5+ years of experience in end-to-end lead generation, outreach campaigns, and appointment setting (High-ticket B2B or Healthcare/Home Care experience preferred).

Proven ability to manage the technical side of cold outreach, including domain procurement, DNS setup (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), and inbox warming to ensure high deliverability.

Expert-level proficiency in for automated sequence management and lead nurturing.

Advanced skills in lead scraping and data mining to build high-quality, targeted prospect lists from scratch.

Ability to manage and scale operations to handle a high volume of leads daily without comproising data integrity or deliverability.

Minimum Technical and Work Environment Requirements:



Internet Connection:

Primary internet connection with a minimum speed of 15 Mbps.

Backup internet connection with at least 10 Mbps.

Backup connection must be capable of supporting work during a power outage.

Primary Device:

Desktop or laptop equipped with at least:

Intel Core i5 (8th generation or newer), Intel Core i3 (10th generation or newer), AMD Ryzen 5, or an equivalent processor.

A minimum of 8 GB RAM.

Backup Device:

Must meet or exceed the performance of an Intel Core i3 processor.

Must be functional during power interruptions.

A functioning webcam.

A noise-canceling USB headset.

A quiet, dedicated home office space.

Peripherals and Workspace: A smartphone for communication and verification purposes.

title: Real Estate Underwriter
description:
About CREx

CREx Software builds tools for commercial real estate investment teams. Our flagship product, Deal Intelligence, is an end-to-end deal pipeline and underwriting platform — covering deal intake, AI-powered lease abstraction, financial modeling, and broker marketplace access across all asset classes.



The role

We're looking for a commercial real estate underwriter who wants to do more than just model deals. In this role, you'll own the customer success function for Deal Intelligence — onboarding new clients, building and validating underwriting templates across asset classes, and becoming the product expert our customers rely on. You know how to underwrite a multifamily acquisition and a triple-net industrial lease in the same afternoon. You're also comfortable giving product feedback and working closely with a small engineering team to make the platform better.



What you'll do

Onboard new customers onto Deal Intelligence — set up deal templates, configure underwriting models, and train users across acquisition and asset management teams



Build and maintain underwriting templates for multifamily, office, retail, industrial/warehouse, self-storage, hospitality, and mixed-use assets



Review and QA AI-generated lease abstractions — verify extracted data against source documents and flag discrepancies for model improvement



Serve as the primary point of contact for customer questions on deal workflow, underwriting logic, and platform usage — resolving issues and escalating bugs



Document workflows, create training materials, and build a customer-facing knowledge base for self-service onboarding



Work directly with our product team to surface customer feedback, test new features, and validate underwriting logic before releases



Assist sales in prospect demos by walking through underwriting workflows and answering real CRE questions in real time

- Resolve issues surrounding bad data submitted by users. Outreach and provide immediate support and responses to stakeholders that provided bad data to resubmit correct data. You will need to adjust your work schedule accordingly to the events needed to support.

- Manage the product listings we have on different e-commerce platform. Support Artwork mockup request by customers.

- Conducting data research to compile list of prospects with qualified data.



**Requirements:**

- Proficient and confident in both spoken and written English.

- Analytical mindset.

- Excellent attention to fine details.

- Able to work independently

- Proficient with computers.

- Experienced with : Canva, Adobe illustrator, 

- Fast internet connection and have backup power generator/battery

- (Bonus) Have experience using sales tools (Lusha)



**Personality:**



- Must be vibrant and highly self-motivated.

- Able to handle a stressful work environment.

- A good team player as we work as a tight-knitted team.

- Confidence in managing multiple projects and deadlines effectively.



If you believe you have what it takes, complete this job application form and we will get back to you asap.

Input job code: 2303



swiy.co/AllForU-Job-Application



Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

title: Senior Backend Manager – DTC Supplement Brand (Operations, CS, Supply Chain, Finance)

You must have hands-on experience working with large, well-known supplement brands and understand how backend systems scale profitably.



This is a high ownership role where you manage the entire backend engine of the company.



Responsibilities

Customer Service

Manage CS team & performance

Improve response times & resolution rate

Reduce refunds & chargebacks

Build retention-focused cancellation flows

Create SOPs for support team

Implement save offers & rebill retention

Retention & LTV

Reduce churn

Optimize subscription flows

Optimize rebill sequences

Winback campaigns

Post-purchase upsells

Work with manufacturers & suppliers

Track CAC vs LTV

Forecast inventory spend

Improve cash flow

Backend Infrastructure

Improve backend funnels

Manage retention tools (Klaviyo, SMS)

Build operational dashboards

Cohort analysis & reporting

Improve backend AOV

Requirements (Must Have)

Experience working with large DTC supplement brands

Experience managing customer service teams

Experience with subscription-based brands

Strong understanding of LTV & churn

Experience with supply chain & fulfillment

Experience managing backend operations

Data-driven decision making

Strong operational mindset

Big Plus

Experience with brands doing $1M+/month

Experience with Loop / Recharge

Experience with Klaviyo retention

Experience managing CS teams

Experience with 3PLs

Experience with COGS optimization

Experience with forecasting inventory

KPIs You Will Own

LTV

Churn rate

Refund rate

CSAT

Response time

Contribution margin

Backend revenue

Subscription retention

AOV (backend)

Inventory turnover

title: Evoto - Volume Photo Editor
description:
Photo Editor Needed — Evoto Required



Need a photo editor with Evoto experience for high-volume portrait editing with 24-hour turnaround.



When applying, include:

 • AWhether you are a company, studio, organization, or solo contractor

 • Your Evoto experience

 • The types of shoots you have edited

 • How many images you process per hour

 • Your internet speed (upload and download)

Work includes school, dance, gymnastics, and cheer portraits: color correction, cropping, retouching, background replacement, cutouts, shadows, mirror effects, and poster creation.



Editing must be done on our Evoto account. Credits are covered.



Pay is hourly or per image, based on speed and quality. First paid test job is $10. Part-time to start.

title: Real Estate Video Editor
description:
Hi! I'm Calven, owner of Cal Media, a modern real estate media company based in the U.S. We create

cinematic listing videos, Instagram Reels, and personal branding content for real estate agents. You can

see our work at 



Job Posting:





I'm looking for a video editor who is hungry, independent, and obsessed with mastering their craft.



? What You'll Be Editing

• Real estate listing videos

• Instagram Reels and vertical social content

• Agent branding videos (talking head + cinematic)

• Social media walkthroughs

We use Final Cut Pro and DaVinci Resolve

? The #1 Thing I Need From You

You must be a self-starter.

I will give you the tools, the software, and the direction. But I need someone who learns fast, figures things

out on their own, watches tutorials on their own time, and constantly pushes to get better. I am not looking

for someone who waits to be told what to do.

I value:

• Honesty — always

• Hard work — every single day

• Independence — own your work

• Mastery — never stop improving your craft

? This is NOT For You If:

• You do the bare minimum and call it done

• You need constant hand-holding

• You're not actively trying to improve outside of work hours

• You treat this as just another job

? This IS For You If:

• You watch videos and immediately think "how could this be better?"

• You study editing styles, color grading, and music on your own time

• You want to grow into a full-time role and take ownership of your work

• You take pride in being really, really good at what you do

?? Position Structure — Apprenticeship Model

• Months 1–3 (Probationary): Part-time hours — $500/month

• Month 4+ (Full-Time): Full-time editor — $1,500/month

The 3-month probationary period is your opportunity to prove yourself. Show me you're a fast learner, that you

care about quality, and that you work independently — and this becomes a long-term full-time role.

? Bonus — Not Required

• Experience with real estate video editing

• Familiar with cinematic edits, color grading, and sound design

• Understanding of Instagram and social media content trends

? How to Apply

Please include ALL of the following — incomplete applications will not be reviewed:

• Start your message with "Cal Media Editor" — if you don't, your application will not be reviewed

• Your portfolio or sample edits (REQUIRED) — no portfolio, no interview

• Your single best edit and why you're proud of it

• What editing software you use

• A short message about why this role is right for you

• A 60–90 second video of yourself answering the following:

1. Tell me who you are and why you want this role

2. What's one video you've edited that you're proud of — and what specifically did you do to make it

great?

3. What do you do on your own time to improve your editing skills?

4. Why do you think great real estate video editing matters to an agent's business?

I'm not just hiring an editor. I'm looking for someone who wants to master their craft and grow with the

company long-term. If that's you — let's talk.



Send all applications to my
 below:

description:
We are hiring for APK VISUALS, a YouTube media company producing and managing multiple faceless channels across TV, Animation, History, and News.



We’re currently looking for skilled Video Editors to create engaging long-form content across our network of channels. This role is suited for editors who can work efficiently, follow direction, and consistently deliver high-quality videos.

title: Hvac Estimator
description:
Job Title: HVAC Estimator

Location: Dallas

Schedule: Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM

Experience Required: Minimum 2 years in HVAC estimating or related role



Job Overview:

We are seeking a detail-oriented and experienced HVAC Estimator to join our team. The estimator will be responsible for preparing accurate cost estimates for residential and/or commercial HVAC projects, analyzing project plans, and coordinating with project managers and technicians to ensure successful bids and proposals.



Responsibilities:

Review project specifications and drawings to determine project scope and requirements.



Calculate labor, material, and equipment costs for HVAC installations, retrofits, and service contracts.



Develop and submit competitive bids and proposals.



Communicate with suppliers and subcontractors to obtain pricing.



Collaborate with project managers and engineers to ensure accurate and timely estimates.



Maintain records of estimates, bids, and completed projects for future reference.



Qualifications:

Minimum 2 years of experience as an HVAC estimator or in a similar HVAC project coordination role.



Strong understanding of HVAC systems, materials, and installation processes.



Proficiency with estimating software (such as FastEST, QuoteSoft, or equivalent) and Microsoft Office.



Excellent math, analytical, and communication skills.



Ability to read and interpret blueprints and technical documents.

title: High-Performance Executive Operator (Data & Operations)
description:
This is not a traditional Executive Assistant role.



We are looking for a high-performance operator who can combine data intelligence with operational execution and work directly alongside a fast-moving, visionary leader.



This role is for someone who can bring structure to data, clarity to decisions, and execution to leadership initiatives.



If you thrive in fast environments, think analytically, and operate with ownership, keep reading.



Who We Are:



DAP Brands is a community of high performers building businesses that create real impact.



Our leadership team moves quickly, thinks strategically, and expects people to take responsibility rather than wait for instructions.



We value:

• Speed

• Accountability

• Emotional maturity

• Precision

• Ownership

• Loyalty to the mission (not ego)



What This Role Really Is:



This role combines Data Analysis + Executive Operations.



Your first responsibility is to bring order to data across the organization: Cleaning, structuring, and translating it into clear insights.



Once the data foundation is organized, you will operate as a high-level executive, helping leadership execute faster and smarter.



You will help:



• Clean, organize, and analyze business data

• Turn raw data into meaningful reports and insights

• Support leadership with decision-ready information

• Protect your leader’s time and priorities

• Enforce accountability across leadership initiatives

• Translate strategy into structured execution

• Identify problems before they become fires

• Move initiatives forward without constant direction

• Bring clarity to complex situations



You will sit close to leadership.

You will see how decisions are made.

You must handle that with professionalism, discretion, and maturity.



What This Role Is NOT



This role is not for someone who:



• Needs constant praise or reassurance

• Struggles with direct feedback

• Prefers slow corporate environments

• Gets overwhelmed by changing priorities

• Waits for instructions instead of solving problems

• Is uncomfortable working with data and systems

• Frequently job hops every year



We operate in a high-accountability environment.



If you need tone cushioning or excessive structure, this will likely feel uncomfortable.



What Success Looks Like (First 90 Days):



Within your first 90 days, you:



• Organize and clean key business data sets

• Create structured reporting for leadership decisions

• Build dashboards or reports that bring visibility to operations

• Run leadership meetings with clear structure and follow-through

• Anticipate your leader’s needs before they are spoken

• Track priorities and ensure accountability across teams

• Bring solutions instead of just raising issues

• Operate calmly and clearly under pressure



The Profile That Thrives Here



You likely:



• Have experience in data analysis, reporting, or business analytics

• Are comfortable working with spreadsheets, dashboards, and structured data

• Have supported a founder or senior executive before

• Have stayed 3+ years in at least one meaningful role

• Enjoy solving complex operational problems

• Can build systems and workflows from scratch

• Communicate clearly and directly

• Are emotionally regulated and professional under pressure

• Think in outcomes and insights, not just tasks



You are someone who:



• Notices patterns others miss

• Brings clarity where others see chaos

• Moves things forward without being asked twice



Compensation & Growth:



This role offers:



• Competitive compensation aligned with performance

• Direct exposure to executive decision-making

• The opportunity to shape internal systems and operations

• Long-term growth as the company scales



At DAP Brands, we reward impact and ownership, not just tenure.



Final Note



This role is for someone who wants to build, solve, and operate at a high level.



If you enjoy working with data, solving operational problems, and supporting leadership execution, this could be an excellent fit.



If you're looking for a comfortable, slow-paced corporate role, this probably isn't it.



But if you want to operate at a high level and help build something meaningful, apply on our website 
/careers and write MAVERICK before your name.

title: Integrations Associate – Automation & Systems
description:
DAP Brands is seeking a detail-oriented, technically savvy, and solution-driven Integrations Associate to support our mission of building seamless, scalable automations across the organization. This role goes beyond task execution. You’ll work closely with our Technology Integration Manager to design, maintain, and optimize workflows that improve efficiency across Sales, Marketing, and Operations.



If you enjoy connecting systems, solving process gaps, and leveraging automation tools to create reliable, intelligent workflows, this role is for you.



Key Responsibilities:

- Integration Setup & Maintenance: Assist in setting up, testing, launching, and maintaining integrations using platforms such as Zapier, Make, and GoHighLevel. Monitor automations for accuracy and performance, troubleshoot issues, and implement long-term fixes.

- Automation & Workflow Optimization: Identify inefficiencies in existing processes and propose smart automation solutions. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand needs and build tailored workflows.

- Technical Support: Work with APIs, webhooks, and basic scripting (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, SQL) to resolve data sync issues, system errors, and performance bottlenecks.

- Documentation & Training: Document workflows, integrations, and processes to ensure scalability. Assist in training internal users on newly built systems and automation tools.



Tools You’ll Work With:

- Automation & Integrations: Zapier, Make, Webhooks

- CRMs & Operations Tools: GoHighLevel, ClickFunnels, Stripe, ClickUp

- Collaboration Tools: Google Sheets, Zoom, Google Workspace

- Scripting & APIs: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, SQL



Application Instructions:

Please submit your application through the online form provided: 
/careers



- To be considered, you MUST type “MAVERICK” in front of your full name if you are applying via our website: 
/careers

- If you are an Integrations Associate who takes ownership, thinks in systems, and thrives in a fast-moving, automation-driven environment, we invite you to apply.

title: Data Analyst (SQL-Heavy / Systems-Focused)
description:
DAP Brands is looking for a high-ownership, systems-minded Data Analyst who doesn’t just report numbers; they build and maintain the foundation that powers decision-making.



This role is critical as we move toward a centralized PostgreSQL database and cleaner, more reliable data across all teams.



If you’re someone who sees messy, inconsistent data and thinks “this needs structure, validation, and automation”. Keep reading.



What This Role Really Is



You are not just analyzing data.



You are responsible for:

- Structuring it

- Cleaning and validating it

- Standardizing it across systems

- Making it usable and trustworthy



You ensure that when leadership reviews data, it’s accurate, consistent, and ready for decisions.



Key Responsibilities

- Build, manage, and optimize SQL/PostgreSQL databases for centralized data access

- Write efficient, scalable queries for reporting and operational use

- Design and maintain ETL processes to ensure clean and consistent data flow

- Perform data cleaning, validation, and normalization across multiple data sources

- Ensure data integrity, especially across CRM and operational systems

- Identify inconsistencies, duplicates, and structural issues in datasets

- Create and maintain client-level dashboards and reporting systems

- Develop reports that directly support business and operational decisions

- Collaborate with teams to define metrics and improve data tracking

- Proactively improve how data is collected, stored, and used



Non-Negotiables (Must-Have)

- Strong experience with SQL (PostgreSQL preferred)

- Strong proficiency in data cleaning, validation, and normalization

- Advanced skills in Excel / Google Sheets (pivot tables, queries, data modeling)

- Experience with CRM systems (e.g., GoHighLevel, HubSpot) and maintaining data integrity

- Proficiency in programming/scripting (JavaScript, XML, or similar)

- Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills

close attention to detail

- Proactive mindset and strong ownership



Strong Plus (Will Set You Apart)

- Experience building or managing centralized databases or data warehouses

- Hands-on experience with ETL tools or automation workflows

- Experience with dashboarding tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, etc.)

- Experience handling client-level or multi-entity datasets

- Background in operations, marketing, or financial data analysis

- Experience in fast-paced, high-growth environments



What We Actually Need (Read This Carefully)



Most candidates can analyze data.



We are looking for someone who can:

- Ensure data accuracy across systems (not just within one report)

- Build clean, standardized datasets fro
ssy inputs

- Maintain data integrity inside CRMs and operational tools

- Reduce manual work through automation and better structure

- Take ownership of data issues and fix them without being told



Final Note



We move fast.

We expect ownership.

We value people who fix problems before they’re told.



If you’re looking to “analyze data,” this role is not for you. Apply on our website 
/careers and write MAVERICK before your name.



Suppose you want to build the backbone of how a company uses data. We want to talk.

title: Content Strategist
description:
DAP Brands is looking for a strategic thinker who understands how content drives growth.



This is not a role for someone who just schedules posts.

This is not a role for someone who chases trends without a strategy.

This is for someone who can connect content to outcomes.



If that excites you, keep reading.



Who We Are



We believe content is one of the most powerful tools to educate, influence, and grow audiences.

But content without strategy is just noise.

We focus on impact, clarity, and purpose.



What This Role Really Is



You are not here to manage a calendar.



You are here to:



Design content strategies aligned with business goals.

Identify high-value topics and narratives.

Coordinate with writers, video editors, and marketers.

Ensure consistency across platforms.

Analyze performance and refine strategy.

You turn ideas into structured content systems.



What This Role Is NOT



This is not for someone who:



Post content without a clear objective.

Chases trends without understanding audience needs.

Avoids analytics and performance reviews.

Content must serve strategy.



What Success Looks Like



Within 90 days, you:



Develop a clear content roadmap.

Align content with marketing and growth initiatives.

Improve engagement and audience development.

Create systems for consistent content production.

You bring structure to creativity.



The Profile That Thrives Here



You likely:



Have experience in content marketing or strategy.

Understand audience development.

Think long-term about brand positioning.

Can connect content to marketing outcomes.

You think like a strategist, not just a creator.



Compensation & Growth



This role includes:



Competitive compensation aligned with impact.

Opportunity to influence brand voice and direction.

Growth as the company and content ecosystem expand.



Final Note



If you understand how content strategy drives brand growth, we want to hear from you. Apply on our website 
/careers and write MAVERICK before your name.



If you only want to schedule posts, this role is not the right fit.

title: Copywriter
description:
DAP Brands is looking for a copywriter who can think like a strategist and write like a builder.



This is not a role for someone who writes generic marketing copy.

This is not for someone who hides behind fluff.

We communicate with clarity, conviction, and purpose.



If that excites you, keep reading.



Who We Are



We build brands that educate, empower, and challenge people to grow.



Our communication style is:



Direct

Clear

Insightful

Strategic



Every word must serve a purpose.



What This Role Really Is



You are not here to simply write content.



You are here to:



Translate ideas into powerful messaging.

Write copy that moves people to action.

Capture the DAP voice across platforms.

Support marketing campaigns with strong messaging.

Refine communication to increase clarity and impact.

Your words shape how the brand is understood.



What This Role Is NOT



This is not for someone who:



Writes generic marketing copy.

Overuses buzzwords and filler language.

Avoids revision and feedback.

Struggles with clear messaging.

Precision matters.



What Success Looks Like



Within 90 days, you:



Capture the DAP voice consistently.

Improve clarity and engagement in marketing messages.

Produce copy that supports campaigns and content strategy.

Your writing becomes instantly recognizable.



The Profile That Thrives Here



You likely:



Have strong marketing or brand writing experience.

Understand persuasion and audience psychology.

Can shift tone depending on the platform.

Write clearly and concisely.

You respect the power of well-crafted words.



Compensation & Growth



This role includes:



Competitive compensation based on output and quality.

Opportunities to contribute to multiple brands.

Direct collaboration with leadership and marketing strategy.



Final Note

title: Real Estate Cold Caller / Acquisitions VA
description:
We are a real estate investment company looking for an experienced real estate cold caller to speak with motivated homeowners, uncover seller motivation, gather key property details, and confidently deliver offers using our proven system.



This is not a generic appointment-setting role.



You will be speaking directly with sellers, having real conversations, qualifying opportunities, and helping move deals forward for property acquisitions.



Our system is already built out. You will not be expected to invent the process from scratch. We provide:

- a step-by-step call structure

- an AI-powered offer calculator

- tailored talking points for offer delivery

- objection-handling guidance

- direct CRM workflow with leads pushed into REsimpli



Your job is to follow the process, sound natural and confident on the phone, and create qualified opportunities.



WHAT YOU WILL DO:

- Cold call homeowners from provided lists

- Build rapport and have real conversations with sellers

- Uncover motivation, property condition, timeline, and price expectations

- Confirm you are speaking with the actual owner / decision-maker

- Use our calculator and AI talking points to deliver offers confidently

- Handle basic objections and keep the conversation moving

- Document clean notes and follow the process correctly in the CRM

- Follow up with sellers as needed



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR:

- Minimum 1 year of real estate cold calling experience

- Experience calling motivated sellers, FSBOs, probate, pre-foreclosure, absentee owners, or similar

- Experience using a dialer and CRM

- Strong English communication skills: clear, confident, conversational, and natural

- Must be comfortable speaking with sellers live and discussing pricing / offers

- Reliable internet, headset, and computer

- Must be available during US business hours (EST or CST)



IMPORTANT:

We do NOT want someone who sounds robotic, overly polished, or overly scripted on calls.

We want someone who sounds natural, confident, conversational, and professional.



This role is a fit for someone who can follow a proven process, use the tools provided, and consistently create qualified opportunities.



PERFORMANCE EXPECTATIONS:

- After ramp/training, minimum 3 qualified offers delivered per week to keep the role

- Strong performers will have opportunities for growth into senior or team lead roles

- Top performers may earn bonuses and long-term advancement



WHAT A QUALIFIED OFFER MEANS:

A qualified offer is not just a casual conversation. It means you spoke with a real owner / decision-maker, gathered the key information, used the process correctly, and delivered a real offer through our system.



WHY THIS ROLE IS DIFFERENT:

We have already built the machine:

- proven workflow

- written SOPs

- AI-assisted offer support

- CRM process

- training and guidance



You are not expected to “figure everything out” on your own.

You are expected to execute well, communicate naturally, and perform.

title: Business Analyst
description:
About the Role

We’re looking for a driven, detail-oriented Business Analyst to join our team and help shape how our business operates and grows. In this role, you’ll go beyond gathering requirements—you’ll play a key part in improving processes, influencing decisions, and helping teams work smarter.



You’ll partner with leaders across operations, manufacturing, finance, customer service, and IT, supporting initiatives that directly impact performance across our U.S.-based manufacturing business. If you enjoy solving complex problems, working with data, and seeing your work turn into real results, this is a great opportunity to make an impact.



What You’ll Do

You will work with business teams to understand and document current processes across manufacturing, order management, finance, and operations. You will identify opportunities to improve efficiency, automation, and data accuracy, and help quantify those improvements using data.



You will translate business needs into clear, detailed functional requirements for IT and project teams, ensuring solutions align with business objectives. You will also support solution design and help ensure systems are practical and user-friendly.



You will develop and execute User Acceptance Testing plans, coordinate testing with stakeholders, and track issues through resolution. You will also support system rollouts, training, and user adoption to ensure successful implementation.



You will serve as a key point of coordination between business users, IT, and the PMO, ensuring clear communication and alignment throughout projects. You will maintain process documentation, support KPI tracking, and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives.



What We’re Looking For

We are seeking candidates with at least 5 years of experience in business analysis, process improvement, or systems implementation. Experience supporting manufacturing or supply chain operations is strongly preferred.



You should have hands-on experience with ERP systems such as Epicor, SAP, or similar platforms, along with exposure to WMS or MES systems. Strong experience with process documentation, requirements gathering, and User Acceptance Testing is required.



Advanced Excel and data analysis skills are important, along with strong organizational and problem-solving abilities. Familiarity with inventory management, forecasting, or S&OP processes is a plus.



You should be comfortable working with U.S.-based teams, including flexibility to support overlapping hours when needed. Strong communication skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple priorities are essential for success in this role.



Why Join Us

This is an opportunity to do meaningful, high-impact work—not just support it. You’ll be involved in initiatives that directly improve how the business operates, from the shop floor to system-level decisions.



You’ll gain exposure to global operations, work with cross-functional leaders, and be part of a team focused on continuous improvement and real results.



If you’re looking for a role where your ideas are valued and your work drives change, we’d love to hear from you.



What Success Looks Like

You help turn complex processes into clear, efficient workflows. Systems are implemented smoothly and adopted by users. Teams are aligned, informed, and supported. And most importantly, your work leads to measurable improvements in performance, efficiency, and decision-making.
